label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern cmp,vp,vpc,naive = W,L,W,L (cmplog AND value_profile_cmplog resolve; value_profile AND naive block) => i2s-PRO single-technique literal-substitution family. The evidence tests verify this independently (G2): they measure the cmplog corpus's literal content (byte-frequency enrichment at a fixed offset, or offset-agnostic anchor-token presence vs depletion), NOT the resolve pattern that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: cmplog (I2S) substitutes a logged CMP literal at a fixed gate offset, so its corpus is enriched in the target value at that offset relative to naive; value_profile/naive cannot synthesize the exact literal

params.applies_to_signatures: operand_kind in {multibyte_literal, single_byte_constant, enum_or_code} AND byte_signature == contiguous_literal AND gate_structure in {equality, switch_case, presence_or_nonnull, length_or_count_check, inequality_or_range}
params.note: lcms/libpng/libxml2/bloaty have no on-disk corpus on s4 (data_realities); their owning server (sB) arbitrates them. Tool returns no_corpus on a server lacking the corpus -> that server leaves the branch inconclusive for this hypothesis, it is NOT decidable:false.

hypotheses.prediction: For a fixed-offset literal value gate, the cmplog corpus is ENRICHED in the target literal at the gate offset relative to naive: signed_target_enrich > 0 (i2s-PRO direction). A pure-coverage / value-profile-only mechanism predicts NO cmplog-specific enrichment at the operand offset (signed_target_enrich ~ 0), because without I2S operand substitution cmplog's corpus byte distribution at that offset would match naive's. This is the alternative the test rules out.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: signed_target_enrich <= 0 (cmplog NOT enriched, or depleted, in the target literal at the gate offset) refutes i2s-PRO substitution at this gate.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Measures corpus byte-frequency at the operand offset (a property of saved seeds), not whether vpc/cmplog resolved the branch. Independent of the Layer-1 resolve pattern.

hypotheses.label: I2S splices one or more logged CMP anchor literals (URL scheme '://', 'HTTP/'/'HTTP/2', header names, protocol tokens 'pop3', chunk-type FOURCCs 'IDAT'/'sRGB'/'eXIf'/'sCAL'/'tIME', OpenType table tags 'GPOS'/'GSUB', the Mach-O magic, the switch comparand byte) somewhere into the input; the literal is PRESENT in the cmplog-resolving corpus but ABSENT (depleted to filler-noise) in the naive/value_profile-blocking corpus, because only I2S operand substitution can plant the exact bytes. The branch is the value-compare itself OR sits one hop downstream of the planted token (dispatch into a handler / scaffold whose downstream state controls the branch).
hypotheses.novelty_vs_refuted: GENUINELY DIFFERENT from the prior refuted 'i2s_multi_literal_scaffold_state_gate' (tool=value_enrichment, an OFFSET-ANCHORED byte-frequency enrichment that data_realities forbid on dispersed/different-sized W/L seeds -> it returned no_gate_signature and was decidable:false). This hypothesis instead uses OFFSET-AGNOSTIC, SIZE-TOLERANT per-seed occurrence COUNTING of named anchor literals with a winner-present / loser-depleted presence RATIO (built tool token_count.py). It does not require a fixed offset or equal-sized arms, so it survives the data_reality that broke value_enrichment.

params.applies_to_signatures: branch has at least one recoverable anchor literal (signature.operand_literal is a printable/hex literal, OR the card names a concrete scaffold token), regardless of byte_signature (contiguous OR dispersed) and regardless of operand_kind. Covers BOTH the dispersed-multibyte scaffold branches AND the contiguous-literal branches that did not clear hypothesis A's offset-anchored enrichment rule (a different measurement, not a relaxed re-test of A).
params.multi_anchor_rule: when a branch lists >1 anchor literal, score each with token_count and take the MAX presence-depletion (best single recoverable anchor) — one planted I2S token present-in-winner/absent-in-loser is sufficient evidence of I2S scaffold assembly.
params.note: Cross-server: curl/harfbuzz seeds on s4; lcms/libpng/libxml2/bloaty seeds on sB. Each server scores its own targets; a server lacking the corpus returns no_corpus -> inconclusive there (NOT decidable:false). data_realities: token_count reads on-disk seed bytes and needs >=1 resolving + >=1 blocking seed for the branch.

hypotheses.prediction: If the branch is reached via an I2S-planted anchor literal, that literal is PRESENT in the cmplog-resolving seeds and DEPLETED in the naive/value_profile-blocking seeds: winner_literal_count >= 1 AND literal_presence_ratio << 1. The alternative this rules out is that value_profile's Hamming gradient (not I2S substitution) assembles the scaffold or that the token is corpus-generic — either predicts comparable presence in both arms (literal_presence_ratio ~ 1), since the gradient is a feedback that cannot place an exact multi-byte token a naive seed also lacks. (Distinct from hypothesis A: A asks 'is the literal enriched at ONE fixed offset'; this asks 'is the literal PRESENT ANYWHERE in the winner yet absent in the loser' — survives dispersed placement + size mismatch.)
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: literal_presence_ratio ~ 1 (anchor literal equally present in the blocking seeds) OR winner_literal_count < 1 (the resolving corpus does not even carry the named anchor) refutes I2S-driven anchor-literal scaffold assembly for this branch.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Reads per-seed occurrence counts of a named literal over the saved resolving vs blocking corpora; never inspects whether cmplog/vpc resolved. Independent of the Layer-1 resolve pattern.

hypotheses.label: The blocked branch is a DERIVED-state condition (accumulated bytecount nonzero, tone-curve float magnitude over a threshold, a deep NULL-pointer guard reached only by traversing a multi-FOURCC chain) with NO single recoverable anchor literal at the gate — I2S still unlocks it upstream, but the controlling quantity is computed program state, not a token the corpus carries.

params.why_no_built_tool: The gate is gated on a value DERIVED at runtime (curl k->bytecount/bodywrites nonzero; lcms tone-curve cmsEvalToneCurveFloat output >= 131072.0; lcms deep LUT-A2B NULL guard; libpng a==0/times==0 fixed-point field). operand_literal is null and the card names no single contiguous token whose corpus presence/depletion the branch turns on. token_count needs a concrete literal; operand_enrichment needs a fixed offset; value_distance_reached needs a target operand value — none is available without per-branch dynamic instrumentation (counting executions reaching the derived condition), which no built measurement tool provides.
params.what_would_make_it_decidable: A depth_reach-style per-branch dynamic tool that runs W vs L seeds through the cov image and reads the derived-condition's hit/side counts (analog of the ctx iteration-depth subtype) — NOT built for these value-derivation gates here.

hypotheses.prediction: (undecidable with built tools) If a derived-state dynamic tool existed, the cmplog-resolving seeds would drive the branch's controlling quantity past its threshold (nonzero bytecount / over-magnitude tone curve / deep-path NULL guard reached) measurably more than the blocking seeds. An alternative where the derived state is reached without I2S would show no winner/loser gap.
hypotheses.g1_falsifiable_by: (no built measurement) — honest inconclusive per G3.

data_realities_respected: I2S is NOT tagged in mutation_op -> no seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence 'I2S event' descriptor proposed.
value_profile (CMP_MAP) is a feedback not a mutator -> its effect is read only via corpus content (byte-freq, token presence), never a lineage gradient event.

joint/structural-assembly gates have different-SIZED W vs L seeds -> hypothesis 2 deliberately uses OFFSET-AGNOSTIC per-seed token COUNTING (token_count), not byte-alignment (operand_enrichment), which data_realities forbid on different-sized seeds.

on-disk corpora exist only for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 (s4) and lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty (sB); each server scores its own targets and a missing corpus yields inconclusive (NOT decidable:false) — cross-server rule honored.
seed-dependent tools need resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds in db/blockers.sqlite for the branch (the reps have them).

kept_priors: i2s_fixed_offset_literal_value_gate (hypothesis A) — KEPT VERBATIM, FROZEN. It confirmed 73 branches via operand_enrichment (signed_target_enrich >= 0.8) and re-validates them unchanged. Its rule and params are byte-identical to the prior. No relaxed re-test of its rule_not_met branches under this hypothesis.
new_mechanism_for_unlabeled: i2s_planted_anchor_literal_presence_scaffold_gate (hypothesis 2) — the NEW discriminating mechanism for the dispersed/scaffold unlabeled branches. Uses the BUILT but previously-unused token_count.py tool (winner-present / loser-depleted presence ratio of a NAMED anchor literal), which is offset-agnostic and size-tolerant, so it works exactly where the prior decidable:false value_enrichment hypothesis B could not. 27 of the 33 unlabeled branches carry a recoverable anchor literal and become DECIDABLE under this test on their owning server: curl (19,21,22,23,118,129_h0,210,298,322,415,416,426,430,441,442,459,567) + harfbuzz (5560,6236) on s4; libpng (3896_h0,3980_h0,4006,4031,4039_h0) + libxml2 (6381) + lcms (2113) + bloaty (141) on sB.

Note this hypothesis ALSO covers the handful of contiguous-literal unlabeled branches that did not clear hypothesis A's offset-anchored enrichment rule (bloaty_141, lcms_2113, libxml2_6381, curl_118, curl_129_h0, curl_210, curl_415, curl_416, harfbuzz_6236): a presence-ANYWHERE count is a genuinely DIFFERENT measurement than A's at-the-offset byte-frequency (a seed can carry the literal off the gate offset, or the W/L size mismatch can defeat the offset-anchored scan while leaving the presence signal intact), so applying it to them is novel, not a relaxed re-test of A.
decidable_false_subgroup: i2s_derived_state_no_recoverable_literal (hypothesis 3) — HONEST decidable:false. 6 branches (curl_73, curl_422, curl_470, lcms_2226, lcms_2330, libpng_3877) are gated on a runtime-DERIVED quantity (accumulated bytecount, tone-curve float magnitude, deep LUT NULL guard, a==0 fixed-point field) with operand_literal=null and no single contiguous anchor token the corpus carries. token_count needs a literal; operand_enrichment needs a fixed offset; value_distance_reached needs a target operand — none available. No built tool can discriminate the I2S-derived-state mechanism here, so per G3 these stay inconclusive rather than be force-labeled. (curl_73/422/470 do carry upstream '://'/'HTTP/' scaffold tokens, but those drive an UNNAMED downstream state, not the gate value itself — counting them would test scaffold presence, which is a weaker proxy; left honestly decidable:false rather than over-claim. An arbiter MAY optionally fall through hypothesis 2 for curl_73/422/470 using their scaffold tokens, but the primary verdict for this subgroup is inconclusive.)
notes.discrimination_summary: Three-tier decision: (1) fixed-offset enrichment (frozen A) for clean contiguous-literal value gates; (2) offset-agnostic anchor-token presence-depletion (new, token_count) for dispersed/scaffold gates and contiguous gates A missed; (3) honest decidable:false for derived-state gates with no recoverable literal. Tiers 1 and 2 are distinguished by the MEASUREMENT (byte-freq-at-offset vs presence-anywhere-ratio), which the data CAN separate; the derived-state tier is non-discriminable by any built tool.
